annotations.label: Orthogonal group O(n)

anchor.snippet: is the group of distance-preserving transformations of a Euclidean space

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the orthogonal group as the matrices whose transpose is their inverse, the matrix realization of O(n).

annotations.label: Special orthogonal group SO(n)

anchor.snippet: called the special orthogonal group

annotations.note: Matrix.specialOrthogonalGroup is the orthogonal matrices of determinant 1.

annotations.label: Orthogonal matrix over a field F

anchor.snippet: is called an orthogonal matrix over F

annotations.note: Membership A ∈ orthogonalGroup ↔ A * Aᵀ = 1 is the orthogonal-matrix condition, stated over any CommRing.

anchors.snippet: is called an orthogonal matrix over F

annotations.label: Orthogonal group of a form

anchor.snippet: the orthogonal group of the form is the group of invertible linear maps that preserve the form

annotations.note: Self-isometries of a quadratic form are formalized but are not packaged as a named orthogonal group of the form.

annotations.label: Orthogonal groups are algebraic groups

anchor.snippet: All orthogonal groups are algebraic groups

annotations.note: No algebraic-group structure on orthogonal groups is established in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Characterization of orthogonal group elements

anchor.snippet: the linear maps from E to E that map orthogonal vectors to orthogonal vectors

annotations.note: This characterization of O(n) elements (up to scaling) is not formalized.

annotations.label: O(n) as norm-preserving endomorphisms

anchor.snippet: consisting of all endomorphisms that preserve the Euclidean norm

annotations.note: The norm-preserving linear automorphisms E ≃ₗᵢ E form a group, which is O(n) for finite-dimensional real inner product spaces.

annotations.label: Euclidean isometry group E(n)

anchor.snippet: Let E( n ) be the group of the Euclidean isometries

annotations.note: The affine isometric automorphisms P ≃ᵃⁱ P form a group, the Euclidean isometry group.

annotations.label: Point stabilizer isomorphic to O(n)

anchor.snippet: This stabilizer is (or, more exactly, is isomorphic to) O( n )

annotations.note: The isomorphism between the stabilizer of a point in E(n) and O(n) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Homomorphism p from E(n) to O(n)

anchor.snippet: There is a natural group homomorphism p from E( n ) to O( n )

annotations.note: The linear-part map from affine isometries to linear isometries exists but is not packaged as a group homomorphism onto O(n).

annotations.label: p is a well-defined homomorphism

anchor.snippet: This is a well defined homomorphism

annotations.note: The linear part is defined and compatible with composition (map_vadd), but no MonoidHom packaging of p is present.

annotations.label: Kernel of p is the translations

anchor.snippet: The kernel of p is the vector space of the translations

annotations.note: Translations are available (AffineIsometryEquiv.constVAdd) but the kernel statement is not proven.

annotations.label: Euclidean group is a semidirect product

anchor.snippet: Moreover, the Euclidean group is a semidirect product of O( n ) and the group of translations

annotations.note: Mathlib has SemidirectProduct generally but not this decomposition of the Euclidean group.

annotations.label: O(n) as orthogonal matrices

anchor.snippet: the orthogonal group can be identified with the group (under matrix multiplication) of orthogonal matrices

annotations.note: Matrix.orthogonalGroup is exactly the group of orthogonal matrices under multiplication.

annotations.label: Determinant of orthogonal matrix is ±1

anchor.snippet: the square of the determinant of Q equals 1

annotations.note: For an orthogonal matrix the determinant lies in unitary R, i.e. det·det = 1 (det = ±1 over ℝ via OrthonormalBasis.det_to_matrix_orthonormalBasis_real).

annotations.label: Special orthogonal group (determinant 1)

anchor.snippet: form a subgroup called the special orthogonal group

annotations.note: specialOrthogonalGroup is characterized as orthogonal matrices with det = 1.

annotations.label: SO(n) is a normal subgroup of O(n)

anchor.snippet: SO( n ) is a normal subgroup of O( n )

annotations.note: No normality instance for specialOrthogonalGroup inside orthogonalGroup is provided.

annotations.label: {±I} is a characteristic subgroup

anchor.snippet: is a normal subgroup and even a characteristic subgroup of O( n )

annotations.note: Subgroup.Characteristic is defined generally but the specific {±I} ◁ O(n) fact is not.

annotations.label: SO(2) is abelian

anchor.snippet: The group SO(2) is abelian

annotations.note: No CommGroup instance or abelianness proof for SO(2) is present (only mem_specialOrthogonalGroup_fin_two_iff).

annotations.label: Canonical form of orthogonal matrices

anchor.snippet: For any element of O( n ) there is an orthogonal basis, where its matrix has the form

annotations.note: The block-diagonal rotation canonical form for orthogonal matrices is not formalized.

annotations.label: Membership in SO(n) via −1 count

anchor.snippet: The element belongs to SO( n ) if and only if there are an even number of −1 on the diagonal

annotations.note: This parity criterion via the canonical form is not formalized.

annotations.label: Euler's rotation theorem

anchor.snippet: is known as Euler's rotation theorem

annotations.note: Listed in docs/1000.yaml as title-only, so not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reflections in O(n)

anchor.snippet: Reflections are the elements of O( n ) whose canonical form is

annotations.note: Reflection in a subspace is defined as a linear isometry equivalence, generalizing hyperplane reflections.

annotations.label: Rotation as product of two reflections

anchor.snippet: every rotation can be decomposed into a product of two reflections

annotations.note: This decomposition is not formalized.

annotations.label: Reflections generate O(n)

anchor.snippet: A product of up to n elementary reflections always suffices to generate any element of O( n )

annotations.note: The generation of O(n) by ≤ n reflections (Cartan–Dieudonné) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cartan–Dieudonné theorem

anchor.snippet: The Cartan–Dieudonné theorem is the generalization of this result

annotations.note: Listed in docs/1000.yaml as title-only, so not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reflection through the origin

anchor.snippet: The reflection through the origin

annotations.note: Reflection in the ⊥ subspace gives the point reflection (negation), a special case of Submodule.reflection.

annotations.label: O(n) is symmetry group of the sphere

anchor.snippet: is the symmetry group of the ( n − 1) -sphere

annotations.note: The identification of O(n) with the isometry group of the sphere is not formalized.

annotations.label: SO(2) isomorphic to circle group

anchor.snippet: is isomorphic (as a real Lie group) to the circle group

annotations.note: No isomorphism between SO(2) and the circle group is formalized.

annotations.label: O(n), SO(n) are compact Lie groups

anchor.snippet: are real compact Lie groups of dimension

annotations.note: No Lie-group or compactness instance for the matrix orthogonal groups is present.

annotations.label: O(n) defined by n(n+1)/2 equations

anchor.snippet: this provides n ( n + 1) / 2 equations that the entries of an orthogonal matrix must satisfy

annotations.note: The count of defining polynomial equations is not formalized.

annotations.label: O(n) has two irreducible components

anchor.snippet: O( n ) has two irreducible components

annotations.note: The component structure of O(n) as a variety is not formalized.

annotations.label: Maximal torus in a compact Lie group

anchor.snippet: A maximal torus in a compact Lie group G is a maximal subgroup

annotations.note: There is no general maximal-torus definition for compact Lie groups in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Form of maximal tori in O(2n), SO(2n)

anchor.snippet: for every maximal torus, there is a basis on which the torus consists of the block-diagonal matrices

annotations.note: The block-diagonal form of maximal tori of O(2n)/SO(2n) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Weyl group of SO(2n+1)

anchor.snippet: The Weyl group of SO(2 n + 1) is the semidirect product

annotations.note: Mathlib has Weyl groups of root systems but not this concrete identification for SO(2n+1).

annotations.label: Weyl group of SO(2n)

anchor.snippet: The Weyl group of SO(2 n ) is the subgroup

annotations.note: The concrete Weyl group of SO(2n) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Low-dimensional orthogonal groups

anchor.snippet: The low-dimensional (real) orthogonal groups are familiar spaces

annotations.note: The topological identifications of low-dimensional orthogonal groups are not formalized.

annotations.label: Fundamental group of SO(n)

anchor.snippet: the fundamental group of SO( n ) is cyclic of order 2

annotations.note: The fundamental group of SO(n) is not computed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stable orthogonal group

anchor.snippet: the homotopy groups of the stable orthogonal group (aka the infinite orthogonal group), defined as the direct limit

annotations.note: The stable/infinite orthogonal group is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sphere as homogeneous space for O(n+1)

anchor.snippet: S n is a homogeneous space for O( n + 1)

annotations.note: The homogeneous-space action of O(n+1) on the sphere is not formalized.

annotations.label: Stabilization of homotopy groups

anchor.snippet: so the homotopy groups stabilize

annotations.note: Stabilization of the homotopy groups of O(n) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Bott periodicity for O

anchor.snippet: From Bott periodicity we obtain

annotations.note: Bott periodicity is listed in docs/1000.yaml as title-only, so not formalized.

annotations.label: Homotopy groups as stable vector bundles

anchor.snippet: homotopy groups of the stable space O are identified with stable vector bundles on spheres

annotations.note: KO-theory interpretations of the homotopy groups of O are not formalized.

annotations.label: π0(O) from orientation

anchor.section: Computation and interpretation of homotopy groups
anchor.snippet: this class survives to O(2) and hence stably

annotations.note: Computation of π0(O) is not formalized.

annotations.label: π1(O) is spin

anchor.section: Computation and interpretation of homotopy groups
anchor.snippet: which is spin comes from SO(3)

annotations.note: Computation of π1(O) is not formalized.

annotations.label: π2(O) vanishes

anchor.section: Computation and interpretation of homotopy groups
anchor.snippet: which surjects onto π 2 (SO(4)) ; this latter thus vanishes

annotations.note: The vanishing of π2(O) is not formalized.

annotations.label: General Lie group homotopy facts

anchor.section: Computation and interpretation of homotopy groups
anchor.snippet: From general facts about Lie groups

annotations.note: These general homotopy facts about Lie groups (e.g. π2 vanishing) are not formalized.

annotations.label: π0(KO) is the dimension

anchor.section: Computation and interpretation of homotopy groups
anchor.snippet: π 0 ( K O) is a vector bundle over S 0 , which consists of two points

annotations.note: KO-theory computations are not formalized.

annotations.label: KO generators from division algebras

anchor.snippet: Let R be any of the four division algebras

annotations.note: The division-algebra description of KO generators is not formalized.

annotations.label: Maslov index interpretation

anchor.snippet: can be interpreted as the Maslov index

annotations.note: The Maslov index is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Whitehead tower of O

anchor.snippet: The orthogonal group anchors a Whitehead tower

annotations.note: The Whitehead tower of O is not formalized.

annotations.label: Sylvester's law of inertia

anchor.section: Of indefinite quadratic form over the reals
anchor.snippet: nondegenerate quadratic forms are classified by Sylvester's law of inertia

annotations.note: Existence is this lemma and uniqueness of the signature is QuadraticForm.sigPos_of_equiv_weightedSumSquares.

annotations.label: Indefinite orthogonal group O(p,q)

anchor.section: Of indefinite quadratic form over the reals
anchor.snippet: The orthogonal group of a quadratic form depends only on the inertia, and is thus generally denoted O( p , q )

annotations.note: Only the indefinite orthogonal Lie algebra so' is formalized, not the group O(p,q).

annotations.label: O(p,q) = O(q,p)

anchor.section: Of indefinite quadratic form over the reals
anchor.snippet: as a quadratic form and its opposite have the same orthogonal group

annotations.note: The equality O(p,q) = O(q,p) is not formalized.

annotations.label: SO(p,q)

anchor.section: Of indefinite quadratic form over the reals
anchor.snippet: The subgroup of the matrices of determinant 1 in O( p , q ) is denoted SO( p , q )

annotations.note: The indefinite special orthogonal group SO(p,q) is not defined.

annotations.label: O(p,q) has four connected components

anchor.section: Of indefinite quadratic form over the reals
anchor.snippet: The group O( p , q ) has four connected components

annotations.note: The component count of O(p,q) is not formalized.

annotations.label: O(3,1) is the Lorentz group

anchor.section: Of indefinite quadratic form over the reals
anchor.snippet: The group O(3, 1) is the Lorentz group

annotations.note: No Lorentz group is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Classification of complex quadratic forms

anchor.snippet: every non-degenerate quadratic form in n variables is equivalent to

annotations.note: All nondegenerate forms over an algebraically closed field of char ≠ 2 are equivalent to a sum of squares, generalizing the complex case.

annotations.label: Complex orthogonal group O(n,C)

anchor.snippet: one associated orthogonal group, usually denoted O( n , C )

annotations.note: O(n,C) is Matrix.orthogonalGroup instantiated at R = ℂ.

annotations.label: O(n,C) has two connected components

anchor.snippet: O( n , C ) has two connected components

annotations.note: The component count of O(n,C) is not formalized.

annotations.label: O(n,C), SO(n,C) as complex Lie groups

anchor.snippet: are complex Lie groups of dimension n ( n − 1) / 2 over C

annotations.note: No complex-Lie-group structure or dimension for O(n,C)/SO(n,C) is formalized.

annotations.label: Equivalence of quadratic forms via congruence

anchor.snippet: two quadratic forms are equivalent if their matrices are congruent

annotations.note: Equivalence of forms is formalized via isometric equivalence rather than explicitly through matrix congruence.

annotations.label: Classification over finite fields

anchor.snippet: are completely classified into congruence classes

annotations.note: Classification of quadratic forms over finite fields is not formalized.

annotations.label: Witt's decomposition theorem

anchor.snippet: Witt's decomposition theorem asserts that

annotations.note: Witt's decomposition theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chevalley–Warning theorem

anchor.snippet: The Chevalley–Warning theorem asserts that

annotations.note: The Chevalley–Warning theorem is formalized.

annotations.label: One orthogonal group O(2n+1,q) in odd dimension

anchor.snippet: there is only one orthogonal group that is denoted O(2 n + 1, q )

annotations.note: Finite orthogonal groups over F_q are not formalized.

annotations.label: Two orthogonal groups in even dimension

anchor.snippet: there are only two orthogonal groups, depending whether the dimension of W zero or two

annotations.note: The two even-dimensional finite orthogonal group types are not formalized.

annotations.label: O^ε(2,q) is a dihedral group

anchor.snippet: The orthogonal group O ε (2, q ) is a dihedral group of order 2( q − ε )

annotations.note: DihedralGroup exists in Mathlib but this identification with O^ε(2,q) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Map (a,b)↦a+αb is a homomorphism

anchor.snippet: the map ( a , b ) ↦ a + αb is a homomorphism

annotations.note: This finite-field orthogonal group homomorphism is not formalized.

annotations.label: Image in O+(2n,q) is cyclic of order q

anchor.snippet: the image is the multiplicative group of F q , which is a cyclic group of order q

annotations.note: This structure result for O+(2n,q) is not formalized.

annotations.label: O−(2n,q) and (q+1)-roots of unity

anchor.snippet: is a group isomorphism from the orthogonal matrices of determinant 1 to the group of the ( q + 1) - roots of unity

annotations.note: This isomorphism for O−(2n,q) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Orders of orthogonal groups in odd characteristic

anchor.snippet: When the characteristic is not two, the order of the orthogonal groups are

annotations.note: Order formulas for finite orthogonal groups are not formalized.

annotations.label: Order formulas in characteristic two

anchor.snippet: In characteristic two, the formulas are the same

annotations.note: Order formulas in characteristic two are not formalized.

annotations.label: Dickson invariant

anchor.snippet: the Dickson invariant is a homomorphism from the orthogonal group to the quotient group

annotations.note: The Dickson invariant of the orthogonal group is not formalized.

annotations.label: Algebraic definition of Dickson invariant

anchor.snippet: the Dickson invariant can be defined as

annotations.note: The algebraic Dickson invariant is not formalized.

annotations.label: SO is kernel of Dickson invariant

anchor.snippet: The special orthogonal group is the kernel of the Dickson invariant

annotations.note: This characterization of SO via the Dickson invariant is not formalized.

annotations.label: Orthogonal groups generated by reflections

anchor.snippet: Any orthogonal group over any field is generated by reflections

annotations.note: Generation of orthogonal groups by reflections (Cartan–Dieudonné) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Reflection in characteristic two

anchor.snippet: A reflection in characteristic two has a slightly different definition

annotations.note: The characteristic-two reflection definition is not formalized.

annotations.label: Center has order 1 in characteristic 2

anchor.snippet: The center of the orthogonal group usually has order 1 in characteristic 2

annotations.note: This center computation in characteristic two is not formalized.

annotations.label: Odd-dimensional char 2 equals symplectic groups

anchor.snippet: orthogonal groups over perfect fields are the same as symplectic groups

annotations.note: The symplecticGroup exists but its identification with odd-dimensional char-2 orthogonal groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Even-dimensional char 2 subgroup of symplectic

anchor.snippet: the orthogonal group is a subgroup of the symplectic group

annotations.note: Matrix.symplecticGroup exists but this subgroup relation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Spinor norm

anchor.snippet: The spinor norm is a homomorphism from an orthogonal group over a field F to the quotient group

annotations.note: The spinor norm is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spinor norm trivial over the reals

anchor.snippet: For the usual orthogonal group over the reals, it is trivial

annotations.note: No spinor norm, hence its triviality over ℝ is not formalized.

annotations.label: Quadratic forms as torsors of orthogonal group

anchor.snippet: quadratic forms over a field can be identified as a Galois H 1

annotations.note: The Galois H^1 / torsor interpretation of quadratic forms is not formalized.

annotations.label: Spin covering short exact sequence

anchor.snippet: The spin covering of the orthogonal group provides a short exact sequence of algebraic groups

annotations.note: The spin-covering exact sequence of algebraic groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Connecting homomorphism is the spinor norm

anchor.snippet: is essentially the spinor norm

annotations.note: This Galois-cohomology connecting map is not formalized.

annotations.label: Orthogonal Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: consists of the skew-symmetric n × n matrices

annotations.note: so n R is the Lie subalgebra of skew-symmetric matrices (mem_so: Aᵀ = -A).

annotations.label: Special orthogonal Lie algebra of a form

anchor.snippet: the special orthogonal Lie algebra consists of tracefree endomorphisms

annotations.note: Skew-adjoint matrices with respect to a form form a Lie subalgebra, but the tracefree/'special' framing is not separately formalized.

annotations.label: Eight inclusions and Bott periodicity

anchor.snippet: are part of a sequence of 8 inclusions used in a geometric proof of the Bott periodicity theorem

annotations.note: The geometric eight-fold inclusion chain for Bott periodicity is not formalized.

annotations.label: Unitary subgroup U(n) of O(2n)

anchor.snippet: U( n ) are those that preserve a compatible complex structure or a compatible symplectic structure

annotations.note: The unitary group is formalized, but its embedding into O(2n) via a compatible complex structure is not.

annotations.label: Orthogonal transforms are conformal

anchor.snippet: real orthogonal transforms preserve angles , and are thus conformal maps

annotations.note: Every linear isometry is a conformal map, covering orthogonal transformations.

annotations.label: Conformal orthogonal group CO(n)

anchor.snippet: The group of conformal linear maps of R n is denoted CO( n ) for the conformal orthogonal group

annotations.note: The conformal orthogonal group CO(n) is not defined as a group in Mathlib.

annotations.label: CSO(n)

anchor.snippet: Similarly one can define CSO( n )

annotations.note: CSO(n) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Discrete subgroups are finite (point groups)

anchor.snippet: discrete subgroups are equivalent to finite subgroups

annotations.note: The finiteness of discrete subgroups of O(n) is not formalized.

annotations.label: 2D finite subgroups cyclic or dihedral

anchor.snippet: the finite groups are either cyclic or dihedral

annotations.note: The classification of finite subgroups of O(2) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Permutation and signed permutation matrices

anchor.snippet: Other finite subgroups include

annotations.note: Permutation matrices are formalized (and are orthogonal), but signed permutation matrices and their role as finite subgroups of O(n) are not.

annotations.label: Pin groups and PO(n)

anchor.snippet: Two covering Pin groups

annotations.note: The Pin group is formalized via Clifford algebras; the projective orthogonal group PO(n) is not.

annotations.label: Spin group and PSO(n)

anchor.snippet: Projective special orthogonal group , SO( n ) → PSO( n )

annotations.note: The Spin group is formalized via Clifford algebras; PSO(n) is not defined.

annotations.label: Cover multiplicities of Spin and PSO

anchor.snippet: Spin is a 2-to-1 cover

annotations.note: The covering multiplicities of Spin → SO and SO → PSO are not formalized.

annotations.label: Stiefel manifold as principal homogeneous space

anchor.snippet: The principal homogeneous space for the orthogonal group O( n ) is the Stiefel manifold

annotations.note: The Stiefel manifold and its torsor structure for O(n) are not formalized.

annotations.label: Incomplete Stiefel manifolds are not principal

anchor.snippet: are still homogeneous spaces for the orthogonal group, but not principal homogeneous spaces

annotations.note: Incomplete Stiefel manifolds and their homogeneous-space structure are not formalized.
